title: Progressive-context manifest (pcx) v0.1
description: A tiered, budget-aware, agent-navigable progressive-disclosure manifest. One file per budget variant; all variants of a source point at the same verbatim leaf store.

usage.description: Self-description of the navigation protocol, so a cold agent that has never seen the format knows how to use the manifest. Shipped at the top of every manifest.

variant.description: Which budget variant this file is. Budgets are arbitrary integers, not a fixed menu.

budget.description: Target context window (tokens) this variant was generated for.

manifest_tokens.description: Token cost of the always-loaded baseline (root + initially-visible frontier descriptors).

siblings.description: Other budgets this source was also built at.

full_tokens.description: Token cost of expanding the entire tree to full leaves. Informational.

nodes.description: All non-root nodes, keyed by id.

what.description: One line: what is behind this node, concretely (names the artifact/action).

when.description: One line: what user goal / question makes this the right branch. Sibling-discriminating, in the user's terms.

desc_tokens.description: Cost of showing THIS node's descriptor in a frontier. Optional; the runtime estimates it from title/what/when if absent.

tokens.description: Cost to expand this node's direct full payload. Branches are 0 (expanding a branch reveals children descriptors instead).

summary_tokens.description: Cost of the optional cheaper 'summary' render level, if the node advertises one.

subtree_tokens.description: Cost to expand everything under this node to full leaves. Lets the runtime plan without fetching.

content_hash.description: Hash of this node's content (leaf) or of its children's hashes (branch). Enables incremental rebuilds — only re-describe changed nodes.

children.description: Child node ids (branch). Mutually exclusive with payload.

payload.description: Pointer to a leaf's verbatim content. Never inlined.

render.description: Available render levels. 'full' is always present and verbatim; 'summary' is an additive convenience, never a replacement.
